resources.string: See all your photos and videos organized by date

The Timeline is your home screen — it displays every photo and video on your device, sorted from newest to oldest. Date headers separate your media by month, making it easy to scroll back to a specific time period. If you have thousands of photos, don\'t worry — Gallery loads them efficiently so scrolling stays smooth.

Scroll through your timeline to browse media. Use the scrollbar on the right edge to jump quickly to any month. You can also pinch to zoom the grid — spread two fingers apart to see larger thumbnails or pinch together to fit more photos on screen.
Open Gallery to see your Timeline
Scroll up and down to browse
Use the right-edge scrollbar to jump to a month
Tap any photo to view it full-screen
Tap to open, swipe to navigate, pinch to zoom

Tap any thumbnail in the timeline or an album to open it in the full-screen viewer. The photo smoothly animates from the grid into full view (if shared element transitions are enabled in Appearance settings). Videos begin playing automatically by default.

Swipe left or right to move between photos and videos without going back to the grid. Swipe down to close the viewer and return to where you were. The viewer remembers your position, so you won\'t lose your place.

Pinch with two fingers to zoom into any photo for a closer look. While zoomed in, drag to pan around the image. Double-tap to quickly toggle between zoomed-in and original size. Tip: if you accidentally swipe to another photo while zoomed in, try the Lock Image feature (long press the date header) to prevent swipes while you explore details.
Long press to start selecting, then tap to add more

Long press any photo to enter selection mode — a checkmark appears on the selected item. Then simply tap additional photos to add them to your selection. For faster selection, keep your finger down after the long press and drag across multiple photos to select them all in one sweep.
Long press a photo to start selecting
Tap more photos to add to selection
Or drag across photos for quick multi-select
Use the action bar to share, delete, or manage

Once you\'ve selected items, a bottom action sheet slides up with all available actions: Share, Delete, Copy to album, Move to album, Hide in vault, and more. You can customize which actions appear and their order in Settings > Navigation > Customize selection actions. Tip: to select all photos in a date group, tap the date header.
Share media with other apps and people

Open a photo or video, then tap the share button in the bottom bar to send it via any app on your device — messaging apps, email, social media, cloud storage, and more. You can also share multiple photos at once by selecting them first in the grid. The share sheet shows your most-used apps at the top for quick access.
Tap the share icon in the bottom bar
Choose an app to share with
Tip: select multiple photos in the grid first to batch-share
Copy a photo to paste it into other apps

Copy a photo directly to your clipboard, then paste it into messaging apps, document editors, email, or any app that accepts images. This is faster than sharing when you just need to quickly drop an image into a conversation or document. The copied image stays on your clipboard until you copy something else.
Tap the copy icon in the action bar
Switch to any app and paste
Tip: works great for quickly pasting into chats
Switch between Timeline, Albums, and Library

The bottom navigation bar is your main way to move around Gallery. It\'s always visible at the bottom of the screen (unless you enable auto-hide in settings). Each tab remembers where you left off, so switching between sections won\'t lose your scroll position.

Timeline shows all your media sorted by date — great for finding recent photos. Albums organizes photos by the folder they\'re stored in (Camera, Screenshots, Downloads, etc.) — perfect for browsing by source. Library is your hub for Favorites, Trash, Vault, Locations, and AI Categories — quick access to everything special.

Albums show your photos organized by the folders they\'re stored in — Camera, Screenshots, Downloads, WhatsApp Images, and more. Each album displays a thumbnail preview and the number of items inside. You can switch between grid and list view to find albums quickly.

Tap any album to see its contents. Long press an album for more options: pin it to the top for quick access, hide it from the list, change its thumbnail, or add it to a group or collection. Tip: pin your most-used albums (like Camera) so they\'re always at the top of the list.
Pin your favorites to the top
Search your photos by name or with AI

Tap the search bar at the top of the timeline to find photos. Basic search matches filenames using fuzzy matching, so you don\'t need to remember exact names. Your recent searches are saved for quick access. Results update as you type.

With AI models installed, search becomes much more powerful — describe what you\'re looking for in plain language like \"sunset at the beach\", \"birthday cake\", or \"dog playing in snow\". Gallery uses on-device CLIP models to understand image content, so your photos never leave your device. To enable this, go to Settings > Smart features and install the AI models.
Switch between modern and classic navigation styles

Gallery offers two navigation bar styles. The modern style shows labeled icons for Timeline, Albums, and Library. The classic compact style uses smaller icons without labels, giving you more screen space for your photos. Try both to see which you prefer.
Quick access to favorites, trash, vault, and more

The Library is your hub for everything beyond the main timeline and albums. From here you can access: Favorites (your starred photos), Trash (recently deleted items, recoverable for 30 days), Vault (encrypted private photos), Locations (photos organized by where they were taken), and Categories (AI-sorted photo groups). Think of it as your gallery\'s control center.
Choose which screen appears when you open Gallery

Choose which screen greets you when you open Gallery. Timeline is great if you mostly browse recent photos. Albums works well if you organize by folder. Library is ideal if you primarily use favorites or categories. There\'s also an Auto option that remembers the last screen you used.
Choose Auto, Timeline, Albums, or Library
Hide the search and navigation bars when scrolling

For a more immersive browsing experience, enable auto-hide for the search bar and/or navigation bar. They\'ll slide away as you scroll down through your photos and reappear when you scroll back up. This is especially useful on smaller screens where every pixel counts. You can control each bar independently.
Toggle \'Auto-hide search bar\' and/or \'Auto-hide navigation bar\'
Scroll down to see them hide, scroll up to bring them back
Light, dark, or follow your system

Gallery supports light and dark themes, and can follow your system setting automatically. The theme affects all screens, dialogs, and menus throughout the app. Most users prefer the system default so Gallery matches the rest of their phone.

System default follows your phone\'s current mode. Force dark mode keeps Gallery dark regardless of system settings — great for nighttime browsing. If you have an OLED display, enable AMOLED pure black for true black backgrounds that save battery and look stunning.
Choose System, Light, or Force Dark
For OLED screens, also enable \'AMOLED pure black\'
Pick any color to theme the entire app

Make Gallery truly yours by customizing its color scheme. System colors match your phone\'s wallpaper-based theme (Material You). Neutral gives a clean, subtle look. Preset palettes offer curated color combinations. Or pick any custom color you like — Gallery will generate a complete theme from it.

The chosen color affects buttons, highlights, icons, cards, and backgrounds throughout the entire app. Experiment with different options to find what suits you — you can always change it later. Tip: System colors update automatically when you change your phone\'s wallpaper.
Go to Settings > Appearance > Color palette
Choose System, Neutral, a preset, or Custom
For custom, tap any color to pick it
Pinch to change how many columns appear

Place two fingers on the grid and pinch in or out to change the thumbnail size. Pinching out (spreading fingers) makes photos larger with fewer columns — great for seeing detail. Pinching in (squeezing fingers) makes photos smaller with more columns — ideal for scanning through lots of photos quickly.

The grid ranges from 2 columns (large thumbnails) to 10 columns (tiny thumbnails for maximum overview). Gallery remembers your preference separately for the timeline and album views. Tip: try 3–4 columns for a good balance between seeing photo details and fitting many on screen.
Pure black backgrounds for OLED screens

If your phone has an OLED or AMOLED display, this mode uses true black pixels which are actually turned off — saving battery and creating a striking visual. Combine with Force dark mode for the best results. Note: this setting only takes effect when dark mode is active.
Enjoy true blacks on your OLED display
Choose the mode that\'s easiest on your eyes

Light mode is clean and bright, best for well-lit environments. Dark mode reduces eye strain at night and saves battery on OLED screens. The system default option automatically switches based on your phone\'s settings, so Gallery always matches the rest of your apps.
Beautiful frosted glass effects throughout the app

Enable blur effects for a modern frosted glass look on the navigation bar, overlays, and the selection sheet. This gives Gallery a polished, layered appearance. Requires Android 12 or later. If you notice any performance impact on older devices, you can disable this.

When enabled, tapping a photo in the grid creates a seamless animation where the thumbnail expands into the full-screen viewer, and shrinks back when you close it. This makes navigation feel fluid and natural. If you prefer instant transitions or experience any animation glitches, you can disable this.
Tap any photo to see the effect
Display as \'Gallery\' or \'Refra\' on your home screen

Choose whether the app shows as \'Gallery\' or \'Refra\' on your home screen and in the app drawer. \'Refra\' is the app\'s alternative name. This changes the icon label only — the app itself stays the same. You may need to restart your launcher or wait a moment for the change to appear on your home screen.
The home screen icon label will update
See your photos organized into monthly sections

Your timeline automatically groups photos by month with clear date headers, making it easy to scroll back to a specific time period. Today\'s and yesterday\'s photos get special labels. The scrollbar on the right edge lets you jump between months quickly. This grouping is always on and works with both grid and mosaic layouts.
Switch between uniform grid and dynamic mosaic

The Timeline offers two distinct layouts. Grid shows uniform-sized thumbnails in neat rows — clean and predictable. Mosaic creates a dynamic layout with varied tile sizes based on photo aspect ratios — more visually interesting and gives you a better sense of each photo\'s composition at a glance.

Try both layouts to see which you prefer. Grid works best with the pinch-to-zoom feature. Mosaic creates a magazine-like feel. Note: mosaic layout currently doesn\'t support pinch-to-zoom but has its own optimized scrolling.
Browse your timeline to see the difference
Stack RAW+JPG pairs, edited copies, and burst shots

If you shoot in RAW+JPG, take burst photos, or edit photos frequently, your timeline can get cluttered with duplicates. Gallery intelligently groups these related images together — they appear as a single item with a small stack indicator, and you can tap to see all group members.

Control which types of photos get grouped. RAW+JPG pairing is great for photographers. Edited copy grouping keeps originals and edits together. Burst grouping stacks rapid-fire shots. Enable only the ones that match your shooting style.
Toggle RAW+JPG, edited copies, or burst grouping
Grouped photos show a stack indicator in the grid
Allow GIFs to animate in the grid

When enabled, GIF files play their animation directly in the timeline grid as thumbnails. This makes it easy to spot and browse GIFs visually. If you have many GIFs, disabling this can improve scrolling performance and reduce battery usage since the animations won\'t play until you tap to view them.
GIFs in the grid will animate when enabled
Remove date headers when browsing an album

By default, albums show date headers just like the timeline. If you prefer a cleaner, continuous grid when browsing albums, enable this option to remove the date grouping headers inside albums. Photos will still be sorted by date, but without the visual separators. This is a matter of personal preference — try both and see what feels right.
Open any album to see the difference
Combine albums from different storage locations

If you have albums with the same name on different storage locations (like internal storage and SD card), Gallery can merge them into a single album. For example, if both your internal Camera folder and SD card Camera folder contain photos, they\'ll appear as one unified Camera album. This is especially useful if your phone saves photos to different locations.
Albums with matching names will combine

Customize how dates appear in the timeline headers. Choose between different formats to match your regional preference or personal style. The format affects all date headers in the timeline, album views, and the media viewer.
Change where the favorite icon appears on thumbnails

The small heart icon on favorited photos can be placed in different corners of the thumbnail, or hidden entirely if you prefer a cleaner look. If the icon overlaps with important parts of your photos, try moving it to a different corner.
Pinch to zoom, drag to pan, double-tap for quick zoom

Use two-finger pinch gestures to zoom into any photo for a closer look at fine details. Double-tap anywhere on the photo to instantly toggle between a zoomed-in view and the original size — great for quickly checking focus or reading small text in photos.

While zoomed in, drag with one finger to explore different parts of the photo. Gallery supports high-resolution zoom so you can see every detail. If you find yourself accidentally swiping to the next photo while zoomed, use the Lock Image feature (long press the date header) to prevent swipes.
Watch videos with full playback controls

Videos play with a full-featured player: play/pause, seek bar, progress tracking, and speed control. Tap the screen to show or hide playback controls. The player supports most video formats including MP4, WebM, and MKV. Audio plays through your device speaker or connected headphones.

Customize your video experience. Auto-play starts videos automatically when you open them. Audio focus pauses your music when a video plays. Auto-hide UI clears the controls when playback starts for a cinema-like experience. All these can be adjusted to your preference.
Toggle \'Take audio focus\' to pause background music
Toggle \'Auto-hide UI on video play\' for immersive viewing
See EXIF data, location, and metadata

Swipe up on any photo in the viewer to reveal the info sheet. It shows key details at a glance: when the photo was taken, file size, resolution, camera model, and GPS location (if available). From here you can also edit metadata, copy the photo, set it as wallpaper, or view it on a map.

For photographers who want the full picture, tap \'View all metadata\' to see every piece of EXIF data: lens info, aperture, shutter speed, ISO, white balance, flash, and much more. This is invaluable for understanding your camera settings and improving your photography.
Navigate through your media with swipe gestures

Swipe left to see the next photo, or right to go back to the previous one — all without returning to the grid. Swipe down to close the viewer and return to where you were in the timeline or album. The viewer maintains your position in the grid, so you\'ll land right back where you started.
Explore panoramic photos with gyroscope panning

Panoramic and photosphere images are automatically detected and open in a dedicated 360° viewer. Tilt your device to look around naturally using gyroscope controls, or drag with your finger to pan manually. This works with standard panoramas, photo spheres, and 360° images.
Play the short video clip embedded in motion photos

Motion photos (captured by many modern phones) contain a still image plus a short video clip of the moment before and after the shot. Gallery automatically detects these and shows a special icon in the viewer — tap it to play the embedded clip. This brings your memories to life with the movement and sound from that moment.
Automatically increase brightness when viewing photos

When enabled, Gallery automatically boosts your screen to maximum brightness while viewing photos in the full-screen viewer. This ensures you see accurate colors and details, especially helpful when viewing photos outdoors or in bright environments. Brightness returns to your normal level when you leave the viewer.
Brightness is boosted only while viewing, not in the grid
View all members of a burst or similar group

When you open a photo that belongs to a group (RAW+JPG pair, burst shot, or original+edit), a horizontal strip of thumbnails appears showing all related images. This makes it easy to compare shots or switch between the RAW and JPG version.
Tap any thumbnail in the strip to instantly jump to that version. This is especially useful for photographers comparing a RAW file against its JPG, or for picking the best shot from a burst sequence.
Prevent accidental swipes while viewing a photo

Long press the date header at the top of the viewer to lock the current image in place. A lock icon appears to confirm. While locked, swiping between photos is completely disabled, so you can freely zoom, pan, and explore every detail without the risk of accidentally switching to another photo.

Long press the date header again to remove the lock and resume normal swiping. This feature is invaluable when you\'re zoomed in on fine details like text in a document, checking focus on a portrait, or examining a detailed landscape.
Open a photo in the viewer
Long press the date header to lock
Zoom and pan freely without accidental swipes
Long press the date header again to unlock
Send photos and videos to other apps

Tap the share button in the bottom bar of the viewer to send the photo or video to any app on your device — messaging, email, social media, or cloud storage. The Android share sheet appears with your most-used apps at the top for quick access. The file is shared in its original quality.
Tap the share icon in the bottom bar
Pick the app you want to share with
Open the built-in editor or use another app

Tap the edit button to open the built-in photo editor, which includes crop, rotate, flip, 20 preset filters, 17 fine-tune adjustments (brightness, contrast, saturation, etc.), and markup/drawing tools. You can also choose to open the photo in an external editor if you prefer. Gallery automatically backs up the original before any edits, so you can always restore it later.
Tap the edit icon in the bottom bar
Choose the built-in editor or an external app
Original is automatically backed up before editing
Move photos to encrypted vault storage

Move a photo to the vault to encrypt it and hide it from your regular gallery. The original file is deleted from your device\'s storage and replaced with an encrypted copy inside the vault. Only you can see it — vault access requires biometric authentication (fingerprint or face) or your device PIN.

You can hide individual photos from the viewer, or select multiple photos in the grid and hide them all at once through the selection action sheet. If you have multiple vaults, you\'ll be asked to choose which one to use.
Choose a vault if you have more than one

Create a duplicate of the photo or video in another album folder. The original stays where it is, and a new copy is created in the destination folder. This is useful when you want the same photo to appear in multiple albums without moving the original. Note: this creates an actual file copy, which uses additional storage.
Choose or create the destination album

Collections let you group related photos from different albums together without moving any files. Unlike albums (which are folders), collections are virtual groupings — perfect for organizing photos by project, trip, event, or any theme you like. A photo can belong to multiple collections.
See complete EXIF data for any photo

View the complete metadata stored in your photo. This includes camera model, lens information, aperture, shutter speed, ISO, white balance, flash, GPS coordinates, software used, color space, and dozens more fields. For photographers, this is essential for understanding and improving your camera technique.
Choose between built-in or external editor

Control what happens when you tap the edit button. Choose \'Built-in\' to always use Gallery\'s editor (great for quick crops, filters, and markup). Choose \'External\' to use a third-party editor like Snapseed or Lightroom for more advanced editing. You can always switch between them.

When set to external, Gallery opens the Android system picker showing all compatible photo editors on your device. Pick your favorite once and it becomes the default. This is ideal if you prefer a dedicated editor for things like HDR adjustments, RAW processing, or advanced retouching.
Videos start playing automatically when opened

When enabled, videos begin playing immediately when you swipe to them or tap to open them. Disable this if you prefer to manually press play, for example when browsing in public or on a limited data connection. Videos will still show a thumbnail preview when auto-play is off.
Hide controls when a video starts playing

When a video starts playing, the toolbar and controls automatically fade away for a distraction-free viewing experience. Tap anywhere on the screen to bring the controls back. This creates a cinema-like feel, especially for longer videos. Disable this if you prefer controls to always stay visible.
Tap the screen anytime to show controls again
Display the date when swiping between photos

The date header shows the capture date at the top of the viewer as you swipe between photos. It helps you stay oriented in time while browsing. Hide it if you prefer a cleaner viewer or if you use the info sheet (swipe up) to check dates instead. Note: the date header is also the target for the Lock Image feature.
The date header also doubles as the Lock Image target
Toggle the favorite button in the viewer

The heart button in the viewer toolbar lets you quickly mark or unmark a photo as a favorite with a single tap. If you rarely use favorites, hiding this button declutters the toolbar. You can still favorite photos from the info sheet (swipe up) or the selection action sheet.
You can still favorite via the info sheet even if hidden
Crop, rotate, and adjust aspect ratio

Use the built-in crop tool to remove unwanted edges, reframe a shot, or focus on a specific subject. Drag the corner handles to adjust the crop area freely, or select a preset aspect ratio for consistent sizing.

Choose from free-form (any size), 1:1 (square, perfect for profile pictures), 4:3 (standard photo), 16:9 (widescreen), and other common ratios. The crop preview updates in real-time so you can see exactly what the result will look like.

The crop tool is the most commonly used editing feature. Tip: use the 1:1 square ratio for social media profile pictures, 16:9 for wallpapers and presentations, and free-form when you just need to trim a specific area.
Open a photo and tap the edit button
Choose an aspect ratio or use free-form

Rotate your photo 90° clockwise with a single tap — tap multiple times to rotate 180° or 270°. Flip horizontally to create a mirror image. These are non-destructive operations — the original is backed up automatically.
Rotation is useful for fixing photos taken in the wrong orientation, or for creative effects. The flip tool is great for selfies if you want to correct the mirrored camera effect.
Open a photo and tap edit
Combine with crop for precise framing
Preset filters, including a one-tap Negative, to transform your photos

Choose from a set of carefully crafted preset filters that transform the mood and tone of your photos. Warm filters (Honey, Desert, Palma) add golden tones, while cool filters (Isla, Clay) add blue or muted tones. The new Negative preset inverts colours in a single tap. Each filter previews instantly so you can compare the effect before applying.

Scroll horizontally through the filter strip at the bottom to browse options. Tap any filter to see a live preview on your photo. If you don\'t like the result, simply tap another filter or tap the original to remove it. Filters can be combined with fine-tune adjustments for even more control.
Open a photo and tap edit
Browse the filter strip at the bottom
Tap a filter to preview it live
Tip: combine filters with adjustments for best results
17 sliders for brightness, contrast, and more

Fine-tune every aspect of your photo with 17 individual sliders: Brightness, Contrast, Saturation, Warmth, Tint, Highlights, Shadows, Sharpness, Vignette, and more. Each slider gives you precise control — drag left or right to adjust the intensity.

Changes preview in real-time as you move the slider, so you always see exactly what you\'re getting. Common workflow: start with Brightness and Contrast for overall tone, adjust Warmth for color temperature, then fine-tune with Highlights and Shadows to recover detail in bright or dark areas. Tip: subtle adjustments often look more natural than extreme ones.
Add drawings and annotations to photos

Draw directly on your photos with customizable brushes. The markup canvas supports zoom and pan, so you can annotate with precision even on small areas. Perfect for highlighting details, adding arrows, circling important areas, or adding fun doodles to photos.

Choose between Pen (solid lines), Highlighter (semi-transparent, great for emphasizing areas), and Eraser (to fix mistakes). Pick any color using the HSV color picker, and adjust the brush size with the slider. The floating toolbar keeps tools accessible while drawing.

Zoom into the area you want to annotate for precision work. Drawing is fully reversible — use the eraser for small corrections or undo to remove the last stroke entirely. The original photo is always backed up before edits.
Open a photo and tap edit
Pinch to zoom into the area you want to draw on
Choose your brush, color, and size
Use eraser or undo to correct mistakes
Choose save format and view edit history

When saving an edited photo, choose your preferred format. JPEG is the most compatible and produces smaller files. PNG is lossless (no quality loss) but creates larger files — great when quality is critical. WebP offers a good balance of quality and file size. You can also adjust the quality slider to control the file size vs. quality tradeoff.

Gallery automatically backs up the original photo before any edit, so you can always restore it later. You can view all your edit backups in a dedicated screen showing the original and edited versions side by side. This safety net means you can experiment freely with edits.
View and restore previous versions of edited photos

Every time you edit a photo, Gallery saves the original version automatically. This means you can always go back to the untouched photo, even after multiple rounds of editing. Backups are stored in the app\'s internal storage and don\'t clutter your gallery.

The Edit Backups Viewer shows all your backed-up originals. You can compare the original with the edited version, restore an original to replace the edited photo, or delete old backups to free up storage space. If storage is a concern, periodically review and clean up backups you no longer need.
Restore originals or delete to free space
Find photos by filename with fuzzy matching

Type in the search bar at the top of the timeline to find photos by filename. Gallery uses fuzzy matching, which means you don\'t need to type the exact name — close matches will still appear. This is great for finding files when you remember part of the name but not the exact spelling.

Search works across all albums simultaneously, so you\'ll find results no matter where the file is stored. Results update as you type for instant feedback. Your recent searches are saved and shown as suggestions when you tap the search bar, making it quick to repeat common searches.
Describe what you\'re looking for in natural language

Search using natural language descriptions like \"sunset at the beach\", \"birthday cake\", \"dog playing in snow\", or \"red car\". The AI understands image content, not just filenames, so you can find photos based on what\'s actually in them. All processing happens on your device — your photos are never sent to the cloud.

Gallery uses CLIP (Contrastive Language-Image Pretraining) models that run entirely on your phone. When indexing is enabled, Gallery analyzes each photo in the background and creates a compact \'understanding\' of its content. When you search, your text query is matched against these understandings to find the most relevant photos.

AI search requires ML models to be downloaded first (about 150 MB). Once installed, Gallery automatically indexes your photos in the background when the device is idle. The initial indexing may take some time depending on how many photos you have, but subsequent updates are fast.
Wait for background indexing to complete

Every search you perform is automatically saved to your history. When you tap the search bar, your recent searches appear as quick suggestions — just tap one to run it again instantly. This is especially useful for searches you repeat often. You can clear individual entries or the entire history if needed.
How Gallery indexes your photos for fast search

Gallery processes your photos in the background to enable fast AI-powered search. Indexing runs automatically when AI models are installed and the device is idle. The first index may take a while for large libraries (thousands of photos), but once complete, new photos are indexed quickly. You can check indexing progress from the timeline — a small status indicator shows when indexing is active.
AI automatically sorts your photos into categories

Gallery uses on-device AI to automatically sort your photos into meaningful categories like Nature, Food, People, Architecture, Animals, Vehicles, and more. This happens in the background with no manual effort — just browse your organized library. All processing stays on your device for privacy.

Find your categories in Library > Categories. Each category shows a preview thumbnail and count of matching photos. Tap any category to see all the photos that belong to it. Categories are sorted by how many photos they contain, with your pinned favorites at the top.
Tap Library in the bottom bar
Tap a category to see its contents

Make categories work for you: create custom categories with your own labels and criteria, adjust the confidence threshold to control how strictly photos are matched, and pin your most-used categories to the top. You can also hide categories you don\'t care about from the categories settings screen.

Beyond the built-in categories, you can create your own with custom names and classification criteria. For example, create a \'Pets\' category, a \'Work documents\' category, or anything specific to your photo collection. The AI will automatically sort matching photos into your custom categories.

Give your category a descriptive name and optionally adjust the confidence threshold — a higher threshold means stricter matching (fewer but more accurate results), while a lower threshold is more lenient (more results but some may be less relevant). Start with the default and adjust based on results.
Adjust the confidence threshold if needed
The AI will start classifying photos automatically
Download or manage on-device AI models

AI features (smart search and auto-categorization) require machine learning models that run entirely on your device — no internet connection needed after download, and your photos are never sent anywhere. The models are about 150 MB and are downloaded once.

Check whether models are installed, download them if needed, or remove them to free up space. After downloading, Gallery automatically starts indexing your photos in the background. You can monitor the model status at any time from this screen.
Wait for indexing to complete after download

Once AI models are installed, Gallery processes your photo library in the background to build an index that powers smart search and auto-categorization. This is a one-time process for existing photos, and new photos are indexed automatically as you take them.

Indexing is designed to be unobtrusive — it runs when your device is idle to avoid impacting performance. A small progress indicator appears in the timeline during active indexing. For large libraries (10,000+ photos), the initial index may take several hours but only needs to happen once.

Albums correspond to the folders on your device that contain photos or videos. You\'ll see Camera, Screenshots, Downloads, WhatsApp Images, and any other folders. Each album shows a thumbnail preview and the number of photos inside. The grid gives you a visual overview of all your storage locations.

Long press any album to reveal its options: Pin it to the top for quick access, Ignore it to hide from both the album list and timeline, change its cover thumbnail to any photo you like, or organize it into a group or collection. Tip: pin your Camera and Favorites albums for quick access, and ignore system folders you don\'t need.
Keep your most-used albums at the top

Pinned albums always appear at the very top of your album grid, regardless of the sort order. This is perfect for your most-used albums like Camera or a specific project folder. You can pin multiple albums — they\'ll all appear in the top section. Unpin anytime by long pressing again.
Tap \'Pin\' to keep it at the top
Long press and \'Unpin\' to remove from top
Ignore albums you don\'t want to see

Ignoring an album hides it completely — it won\'t appear in the Albums tab, and its photos won\'t show up in the Timeline. This is useful for system folders (like \'.thumbnails\'), app-generated folders, or albums with media you don\'t want cluttering your gallery. The files aren\'t deleted, just hidden from Gallery.
Ignored album photos also disappear from Timeline

Changed your mind? Go back to Settings > Ignored albums at any time to see all your hidden albums and restore any of them. Restored albums immediately reappear in the Albums tab and their photos return to the Timeline. No data is lost when ignoring or restoring.
Create album groups for better organization

If you have many albums, groups help you organize them visually. For example, group all social media folders together, or keep work-related albums separate from personal ones. Groups appear as collapsible sections in your album list, making it much easier to find what you need.

Long press any album and tap \'Add to group\'. Create a new group name or add to an existing one. You can add multiple albums to the same group. Groups can be collapsed to save space when you don\'t need them.
Name your group or pick an existing one
Add more albums to the same group
Group related albums into curated collections

Collections are virtual groupings that let you bring together photos from different albums under a single theme — a vacation trip, a home renovation project, a recipe collection, or anything you want. Unlike albums (which are tied to folders), collections span across your entire library.

Start a collection from any album or photo. Add photos from any source — they don\'t need to be in the same folder. The same photo can belong to multiple collections without creating copies, so there\'s no extra storage cost. Browse your collections from the Library screen.
Long press an album or select photos
Name your collection or pick an existing one
Choose a specific photo as the album cover

By default, albums show their most recent photo as the cover. You can override this with any photo from the album to create a more representative or visually appealing cover. This is great for making your album grid look organized and easy to scan at a glance.

Open the album, find the photo you want as the cover, then long press it and select \'Set as album thumbnail\'. The album grid will immediately update with your chosen cover. To reset to the default (most recent photo), you can set a different thumbnail or clear it from album options.
Encrypt and protect your private photos

The vault is a secure, encrypted space for photos and videos you want to keep private. Files in the vault are encrypted using strong cryptography and can only be accessed after biometric authentication (fingerprint or face) or your device PIN. They\'re completely hidden from your regular gallery and won\'t appear in the timeline, albums, or any other app.

Setting up the vault is quick — just tap Library > Vault and follow the prompts. You can create multiple vaults for different purposes (e.g., \'Personal\' and \'Documents\'). Each vault requires authentication every time you access it.
Verify with your fingerprint, face, or PIN

Move photos to the vault from the viewer (three-dot menu > Hide in vault) or by selecting multiple photos and using the selection action sheet. When you move a photo to the vault, the original is deleted from your gallery and replaced with an encrypted copy. Only vault access can reveal it.
Hide photos by moving them to encrypted storage

Moving a photo to the vault encrypts it and removes it from your visible gallery. The encrypted file is stored in the app\'s secure storage, inaccessible to other apps or file managers. This is the safest way to keep private photos on your device.

From the viewer: tap the three-dot menu and select \'Hide in vault\'. From the grid: select one or more photos, then use the \'Hide in vault\' action in the selection sheet. You can hide photos, videos, or a mix of both. If you have multiple vaults, you\'ll be asked which one to use.
Open a photo, or select multiple in the grid
Tap the menu or selection action
Choose a vault if you have more than one
Move photos back to your regular gallery

To bring photos back from the vault to your regular gallery, open the vault, select the photos you want to restore, and tap the restore action. The files are decrypted and placed back in their original location (or a default folder if the original no longer exists). Once restored, the photos reappear in your timeline and albums as if they were never hidden.
Watch encrypted videos without full decryption

Videos stored in the vault play using streaming decryption, meaning they start playing almost immediately without needing to decrypt the entire file first. This is especially important for long or large video files that would otherwise take a long time to prepare. The decryption happens on-the-fly as the video plays, with no temporary unencrypted files created on disk.
Mark your best photos for quick access

Tap the heart icon in the viewer toolbar to mark a photo as a favorite. Favorites are synced through Android\'s MediaStore, so they persist even if you switch gallery apps. Access all your favorited photos and videos from Library > Favorites. Tip: you can also favorite multiple photos at once by selecting them and using the selection action sheet.
See all your favorite photos in one place

The Favorites screen in the Library shows all your starred photos and videos in one place, organized by date just like the timeline. This is a great way to quickly access your best shots, important documents, or any photos you want to find again easily. The heart icon\'s position on thumbnails can be customized in Settings > Timeline & Albums.
Deleted photos go to trash first

When you delete photos, they go to the trash first rather than being permanently removed. Items stay in the trash for 30 days, giving you a safety net to recover accidentally deleted photos. After 30 days, they\'re automatically and permanently deleted. This behavior mirrors Android\'s built-in trash system.

Go to Library > Trash to see all trashed items with their remaining days until permanent deletion. From here you can restore individual items back to their original location, permanently delete specific items immediately, or empty the entire trash at once. Tip: check the trash before performing a factory reset or clearing app data.
View items and their remaining days
Select items to restore or permanently delete
Restored items return to their original album
Control whether deleted items go to trash

If you prefer permanent deletion without the 30-day trash period, you can disable the trash can entirely. When disabled, deleted photos are removed immediately and cannot be recovered. This saves a small amount of storage but removes the safety net. Recommendation: keep the trash enabled unless you have a specific reason to disable it.
When off: deletions are immediate and permanent
When on: deleted items go to trash for 30 days
See your photos on a map

View your photos organized by the places where they were taken. Gallery reads GPS coordinates from your photo metadata and groups them by city, neighborhood, or landmark. This is a wonderful way to revisit trips, explore where you\'ve been, and discover photo clusters you might have forgotten about.

Tap any location to see all the photos taken there, displayed in a timeline view. If you have Mapbox maps enabled, you can also see an interactive map with pins showing exactly where each photo was captured. Note: location data depends on your camera saving GPS info — most phone cameras do this by default.
See where a photo was taken from the info sheet

Swipe up on any photo in the viewer to reveal the info sheet. If the photo contains GPS metadata, you\'ll see the location displayed with an address. Tap it to open a map showing exactly where the photo was taken. You can also edit or delete the location data for privacy (see the Metadata section for details).
See camera, date, resolution, and location info

Swipe up on any photo in the viewer to see its metadata at a glance: capture date and time, resolution, file size, camera model, and GPS location. Gallery collects and caches this metadata in the background for fast access. This info helps you understand when, where, and how each photo was taken.

Tap \'View all metadata\' to see every detail embedded in the photo file: focal length, aperture, shutter speed, ISO, white balance, metering mode, color space, software version, and much more. This is invaluable for photographers learning to improve their technique or for verifying photo authenticity.
Change date, location, and other metadata

Gallery lets you modify certain photo metadata fields directly. You can change the capture date (useful for scanned photos or when the camera date was wrong) and GPS location (to correct or add location info). Changes are written directly to the file\'s EXIF data.

Open a photo, swipe up to see the info sheet, and tap the edit icon next to the field you want to change. A dialog lets you enter the new value. Be careful when editing — changes are permanent and overwrite the original metadata. Tip: use this to fix incorrect dates on old scanned photos.
Open a photo and swipe up
Tap the edit icon next to date or location
Changes are saved directly to the file
Remove location data from photos for privacy

For privacy, you can strip GPS coordinates from any photo. This is recommended before sharing photos publicly or on social media if you don\'t want to reveal where they were taken. The deletion is permanent — the GPS data is removed from the file\'s EXIF metadata and cannot be recovered.
Open a photo and swipe up
GPS data is permanently removed from the file

If photo details appear outdated or missing (e.g., after restoring from backup or editing outside Gallery), trigger a full metadata re-scan. This re-reads all EXIF data from your photo files and updates Gallery\'s metadata cache. The scan runs in the background and may take a few minutes for large libraries.
Wait for the background scan to complete
Photo details will update as they\'re re-read
System, neutral, or preset color themes

Choose how Gallery looks. System colors match your phone\'s Material You wallpaper theme. Neutral provides a clean, muted appearance. Presets offer a selection of curated color combinations, each generating a full light and dark theme.

The chosen color flows through the entire app — buttons, cards, icons, selections, and backgrounds all coordinate. Changes apply instantly so you can preview the result. Experiment freely to find what feels right for you.
Go to Settings > Appearance > Color palette

Override your system theme to keep Gallery in dark mode at all times, regardless of your phone\'s system-wide setting. This is useful if you prefer dark mode in Gallery even when your phone is in light mode. Combine with AMOLED pure black for the ultimate dark experience on OLED screens.
Gallery stays dark even when your phone is in light mode
Pure black backgrounds for OLED screens

On OLED and AMOLED displays, true black pixels are completely turned off, saving battery and creating a stunning high-contrast look. This setting replaces dark gray backgrounds with pure black. Note: it only takes effect when dark mode is active (either system dark mode or Force dark mode).
Enable dark mode or Force dark mode
Control whether deleted items go to trash first

Controls the safety net for deletions. When enabled (recommended), deleted items go to trash for 30 days before permanent deletion, giving you time to recover mistakes. When disabled, all deletions are instant and permanent — use with caution.
Ask for confirmation before trashing items

Adds an extra confirmation dialog before moving items to trash. This prevents accidental deletions, especially useful if you sometimes hit the delete button by mistake. When disabled, items are trashed immediately without asking.
Recommended if you accidentally delete often
Hide from recents and block screenshots

When activated, Gallery is hidden from the recent apps screen (the task switcher shows a blank preview) and screenshots within the app are blocked. This is an extra privacy measure if you don\'t want others to see what you were browsing when they glance at your recent apps. Ideal when combined with the vault for maximum privacy.
Screenshots are blocked within the app
Haptic feedback for selections and actions

Controls haptic feedback (subtle vibration) for interactions like long pressing to select, toggling favorites, and other actions. Haptic feedback makes the app feel more responsive and tactile. Disable if you prefer a completely silent experience or if vibrations bother you.
Affects long press, selection, and other interactions
Choose Auto, Timeline, Albums, or Library

Choose which screen greets you when you open Gallery. Auto remembers the last screen you used. Timeline is great for browsing recent photos. Albums is ideal if you organize by folder. Library works well if you primarily access favorites, vault, or categories.
Choose Auto, Timeline, Albums, or Library
Use the compact navigation bar style

The modern style shows labeled icons (Timeline, Albums, Library) for clarity. The classic compact style uses smaller icons without labels, giving you more vertical screen space for photos. Try both to see which feels more comfortable for your workflow.
Modern: labeled icons, Classic: compact icons
Display titles in the selection action sheet

When you select photos, the bottom action sheet shows icons for available actions. Enabling titles adds text labels under each icon (Share, Delete, Copy, etc.) for clarity. Disabling titles gives a cleaner, more compact look. If you already know what each icon does, hiding titles saves space.
With titles: labels under each action icon
Reorder and customize the selection action sheet

The selection action sheet appears whenever you select photos. By default it shows Share, Delete, Copy, Move, Hide in vault, and more. You can customize which actions appear and their order to match your most common workflows.

Drag actions up or down to reorder them — put your most-used actions at the top for quick access. Toggle actions on or off to show or hide them. For example, if you never use \'Copy to album\', hide it to reduce clutter. Your customization is saved automatically.
Drag actions to reorder by priority
Download and manage AI model status

View the current status of AI models: whether they\'re installed, downloading, or not yet set up. The models are required for AI-powered search and auto-categorization. They\'re downloaded once (~150 MB) and run entirely on your device.

Download models to enable AI features, check their status, or remove them to free storage. After downloading, Gallery automatically begins indexing your photos in the background. If models are already bundled with your app version, they\'ll be copied from assets on first launch.
View and manage edit backup storage

Every time you edit a photo, Gallery saves the original version. This screen shows all your backups with their size and date. You can compare originals with edited versions side by side to see exactly what changed.

Backups can accumulate over time, especially if you edit many photos. Review your backups periodically and delete ones you no longer need to free storage. You can delete individual backups or clear them all at once. Restoring a backup replaces the edited photo with the original.
Browse, compare, restore, or delete backups

Place two fingers on the photo grid and pinch in or out to change the column count. Pinching out (spreading fingers) reduces columns for larger thumbnails — great for seeing details. Pinching in (squeezing fingers) adds columns for smaller thumbnails — ideal for scanning lots of photos. The transition is smooth and animated.

Grid ranges from 2 columns (very large thumbnails) to 10 columns (tiny thumbnails for maximum overview). Gallery remembers your preference separately for the timeline and album views, so you can use different sizes in each. Tip: 3–4 columns is a good default for most people.
Swipe between photos in the viewer

In the full-screen viewer, swipe left to see the next photo and right to go back. Swipe down to close the viewer and return to the grid. All swipe gestures are smooth and responsive, with the next photo sliding in as the current one slides out. The viewer remembers your grid position, so closing always brings you back to where you were.
Start selection mode with a long press

Long press (hold your finger down for about half a second) on any photo to enter selection mode. A checkmark appears on the selected item, and you can then tap additional photos to add them. To exit selection mode, use the back button or tap the \'X\' to deselect all. If haptic feedback is enabled, you\'ll feel a subtle vibration when selection activates.
Drag across photos to select multiple

After long pressing to start selection, keep your finger down and drag across multiple photos in any direction to select them all in one continuous motion. This is much faster than tapping each photo individually, especially when you need to select a large group of consecutive photos. Works across rows and columns.

When you select one or more photos, a bottom action sheet slides up showing all available actions: Share, Delete, Copy to album, Move to album, Hide in vault, Favorite, and more. The number of selected items is displayed at the top. Tap any action to apply it to all selected items at once.

The action sheet is fully customizable. Reorder actions to put your most-used ones first, and hide actions you never use. This makes the sheet cleaner and faster to use. Your customization is saved permanently.
Drag to reorder, toggle to show/hide
Most-used actions should be at the top

Select multiple photos in the grid, then tap the Share action to send them all at once to any app. This is perfect for sharing a group of vacation photos, sending multiple documents, or uploading a batch to cloud storage. All selected items are shared as a single bundle.
Tap the Share action in the sheet
Choose the app to share with

Select multiple photos, then tap Delete to move them all to trash at once. If trash confirmation is enabled, you\'ll be asked to confirm. If the trash is enabled, items can be recovered within 30 days from Library > Trash. This is the fastest way to clean up unwanted photos from a specific event or duplicates.
Tap photos to add to selection
Tap the Delete action in the sheet
Items go to trash (recoverable for 30 days)
Gallery respects your system font size

Gallery fully respects your system font size settings. If you\'ve increased the text size in Android\'s accessibility settings for easier reading, all text in Gallery — labels, headers, descriptions, buttons — will scale accordingly. No additional configuration needed within Gallery.
Use Gallery when other apps request a photo

When other apps ask you to pick a photo (for example, setting a profile picture or attaching an image to a message), Gallery can serve as your media picker. You\'ll see a familiar Gallery interface to browse and select photos, instead of the default Android picker. This gives you access to all your albums and search features even when picking from another app.
View photos sent from other apps

When you tap an image or video link in a browser, file manager, or any other app, Gallery can open it as a standalone viewer. You get the full viewing experience — zoom, pan, video playback, and info sheet — even for files that aren\'t in your regular gallery. Set Gallery as your default image viewer in Android settings for the best experience.
Use any photo as your device wallpaper

Open any photo and use the action menu to set it as your device wallpaper. You can choose to set it for the home screen, lock screen, or both. Gallery opens a crop interface so you can position and resize the photo to fit your screen perfectly before applying.
Open a photo in the viewer
Choose home screen, lock screen, or both
Stream media to FCast-compatible devices on your network

Cast videos and images to any FCast-compatible device on your local network. Gallery discovers devices automatically using mDNS, sets up a local streaming server, and plays your media on the big screen — all without any cloud services. Encrypted vault media can also be cast securely via temporary decryption.

While casting, a mini controller appears in the viewer showing cast status. Use the remote controls to play, pause, seek, adjust volume, and change playback speed — all from your phone. Tap the cast button again to stop casting or switch devices.
Open a video in the viewer
Tap the cast button in the top bar
Select a device from the picker
Use on-screen controls to manage playback
Automatically enhance display contrast based on image brightness

When enabled, Gallery analyzes the brightness of each photo in real time and automatically adjusts the display contrast to make dark images easier to see and bright images more vivid. The adjustment is subtle and happens instantly as you swipe between photos. This only affects how images look on screen — your original files are never modified. This feature is currently in Beta.
Swipe between photos to see real-time adjustments
Move action buttons to the right side for easier reach

When enabled, the top action buttons in the selection bar (Select All, Details) are pushed to the right edge of the screen while the Close button stays on the left. This makes the actions easier to reach with one hand, especially on larger phones. The buttons are placed in a scrollable row to prevent overflow.
Go to Settings > Navigation > Selection actions
Select items to see the new layout
Gate authentication, custom passwords, and a streamlined vault flow

Add an extra layer of security before the vault selector screen. Choose between no gate, device security (biometrics or screen lock), or a custom password. When active, you must authenticate before you can even see the list of available vaults — preventing others from knowing which vaults exist on your device.
Open the Vault from the Library
Choose None, Device Security, or Custom Password

Each vault now supports its own authentication — biometrics, PIN, or a custom password — so you can have different security levels for different vaults. The vault creation flow has been streamlined: create a vault, set its password, then configure the global gate. Vault deletion now returns you to the selector with re-authentication required, and the last vault deletion exits to the library.
Set a custom password or use device security
Each vault can have its own independent security
Isolate untrusted file processing in secure sandbox processes

Sandboxing protects the app by processing untrusted files — such as HEIF, AVIF, and JXL images — in isolated system processes. If a malicious file exploits a decoder vulnerability, the damage is contained in the sandbox and cannot reach the main app or your data.

You can control how aggressively files are sandboxed. Metadata isolation determines whether EXIF parsing happens in a shared process, per-file process, or a hybrid of both. Sandboxed image decoding routes HEIF/AVIF/JXL through an isolated decoder service using shared memory.
Choose a metadata isolation mode (Shared, Hybrid, or Per-file)
Toggle sandboxed image decoding on or off
Protect your settings and database with Android Keystore encryption

When enabled, your app preferences are encrypted using AES-256-GCM with keys stored in the Android Keystore. The Room database is also encrypted using SQLCipher, protecting your trash index, categories, and internal data at rest — even if the device is rooted.
Encryption is opt-in. Enabling it triggers a one-time migration that encrypts your existing data. This runs in the background and only needs to happen once.
The app will migrate your data automatically
Keep sensitive files hidden and protected via Android\'s Storage Access Framework

The private folder uses Android\'s Storage Access Framework (SAF) to store files outside of MediaStore. Files in your private folder never appear in the normal timeline, are not indexed by other apps, and are protected behind the app\'s security gate.
Pick any folder on your device as the private folder. The app takes persistent access permission so it survives reboots. You can browse, view, and manage private files just like regular media.
Tap Private folder and select a directory
Access your private files from the Library
Filter your timeline by type, favorites, year, or album

The timeline filter lets you narrow down what you see without leaving the main screen. Filter by media type (photos, videos, GIFs), show only favorites, jump to a specific year, or scope the timeline to a single album. Combine multiple filters for precise results.
Access the filter directly from the search bar at the top of the timeline. Tap the filter icon to expand filter options. Active filters are shown as chips that can be individually removed.
Tap the filter icon in the search bar
Select a filter category (type, favorites, year, album)
Tap a filter chip to remove it
The filter visibility toggle is available in Settings
View animated AVIF, JXL, and APNG images

Gallery now supports animated AVIF, animated JXL, and APNG image formats. These modern animated image formats offer better compression and quality compared to GIF. Animated images play automatically in the media viewer just like GIFs.

The following animated formats are supported alongside traditional GIF: Animated AVIF (AV1-based animation), Animated JXL (JPEG XL animation), and APNG (Animated PNG). Thumbnail animation in the grid can be controlled from the Timeline & Albums settings.
Select embedded or external subtitle tracks for videos

Gallery supports both embedded subtitle tracks (built into the video file) and external subtitle files (.srt, .ass, .ssa, etc.). When a video has subtitle tracks available, a subtitle button appears in the video player controls.

Tap the subtitle button during video playback to open the subtitle selector. You can switch between available tracks, load an external subtitle file, or disable subtitles entirely. External subtitle files can also be deleted from the bottom sheet.
Tap the subtitle icon in the video player
Select an embedded track or load an external file
Zoom into videos with pinch and double-tap gestures

You can now pinch-to-zoom and double-tap-to-zoom while watching videos, just like with photos. This is useful for examining fine details in video footage. Pan around while zoomed in by dragging with one finger. Double-tap again to reset to the original size.
Find photos by their description or metadata text

Search now extends beyond filenames and AI analysis. Your search queries also match against image descriptions, EXIF metadata text fields, and other embedded metadata. This makes it easier to find photos you have annotated or that contain specific camera or lens information.
Search for albums when copying or moving photos

When copying or moving photos to another album, you can now search for the target album by name. A search bar at the top of the album picker sheet lets you quickly filter the album list, making it much faster to find the right destination when you have many albums.
Refreshed screens for Trash and Favorites

The Trash and Favorites screens have been completely redesigned with a fresh layout, improved visual hierarchy, and smoother animations. The new design brings them in line with the modern look of the rest of the app.
Swipeable photo stories above the timeline

Story cards appear as a horizontal carousel above your timeline, surfacing highlights from your recent photos, albums, categories, locations, and favorites. Tap any card to open an immersive full-screen story viewer with auto-advancing slides and progress indicators — just like stories in social media apps.

Control which card types are shown and their order. You can toggle memory, album, category, location, and favorites cards individually, reorder them by drag, and configure the story viewer behavior including auto-advance and duration per photo.
Go to Settings > Timeline & albums > Story cards
Toggle individual card types on or off
Compact animated popup for video controls

Video control buttons (subtitle, speed, volume, rotate) have been consolidated into a single \'more options\' button that expands into a blurred animated popup. The popup uses a row layout in landscape and column in portrait, keeping the video view uncluttered while still providing quick access to all controls.
Swipeable photo stories above the timeline with auto-advancing viewer and configurable card types
Animated more-options popup replaces the tall stack of video control buttons
Auto-contrast and adaptive colors now extend to the video options popup
Cold startup optimized from ~900 ms to ~350 ms with lazy keystore caching and deferred initialization
More accurate photo-to-viewer transitions with single stable registration and Long-based keys

Fixed move/copy album access with MANAGE_MEDIA, screen rotation reset on video exit, status bar sync, ClassCastException after unlock, grid realignment on back navigation, loading shimmer during startup, and empty-media padding
Filter the timeline by media type, favorites, year, or album
Play animated AVIF, JXL, and APNG images alongside GIF
Embedded and external subtitle track selection with bottom-sheet management
Pinch-to-zoom and double-tap-to-zoom during video playback
Search through image descriptions, metadata text fields, and inline timeline filter
Refreshed layout, improved visual hierarchy, and smoother animations
Search bar in the copy/move album picker for quick album lookup
Dismissible settings banners, bottom-sheet confirmations, and optimistic trash feedback
Fixed drag-selection tracking, metadata edit keyboard overlap, and preserved original dates when overriding edited photos
App sandbox (targetSandboxVersion 2), encrypted storage, and isolated file processing
HEIF, AVIF, and JXL images are decoded in isolated processes for exploit protection
EXIF and metadata extraction runs in per-file sandbox processes
Store sensitive files outside MediaStore via SAF, hidden behind a security gate
Encrypt app preferences (AES-256-GCM) and Room database (SQLCipher) at rest
MediaStore rescan state is now persisted in the database across app restarts
Lazy per-album timeline flows, reduced recompositions in media viewer, optimized collection types and state management
Fixed slow AI classification, dark status bar icons, map intent crash, motion player leak, large batch copy crash, filename date fallback, and updated dependencies
Cast videos and images to devices on your local network
Real-time luminance-based contrast enhancement in the viewer
Move top action buttons to the right for easier one-handed use
Gate authentication, custom passwords, per-vault security, and streamlined flow
New build variant with AI models but without online features (maps, cloud, network)
Fixed phantom selections, corrected setup permissions, and improved image loading
Automatically combine albums that share the same name
Consolidate nested sub-folder albums into their parent
Precise video scrubbing with adjustable sensitivity
Find visually similar photos using any image as a query
Dynamic mosaic tile layout for the timeline
Create custom albums by grouping photos from anywhere
Complete UI and UX overhaul for all settings screens
New unified button design used throughout the app
Redesigned categories screen with improved navigation
Customize which actions appear and their order
Fine-tune which similar photos are grouped together
AI models can now be downloaded on demand instead of bundled
Fixed video codec exhaustion, favorite button in timeline, and grouped media selection
View all EXIF and metadata for images and videos
Enhanced photo editor with text overlay support
Migrated from Mapbox to open-source MapLibre
Stack RAW+JPG, edited copies, and burst shots
Choose built-in or external image editor
Option to disable GIF animation in grid view
View and manage edit history backups
Copy photos to clipboard for pasting
Zoom, pan, and floating drawing tools
Customize favorite icon placement on thumbnails
Search photos using natural language descriptions
Personalize app colors with wallpaper themes or curated presets
Detect and play motion photo clips
Browse your photos on an interactive map by location
Set any photo as the cover for your albums
Revamped selection actions with addons and select-all
Rebuilt search experience with improved results and history
View your albums in a compact list layout
Lock the current photo to prevent accidental swipes
Fixed encrypted video streaming for reliable playback
Polished interface with refined animations and layouts
Group by day, month, or year
Choose how each screen groups media — and set it per screen

Pick how your media is grouped under date headers: a flat list, by day, by month, or by year. Bigger groups make it faster to scan a large library, while daily grouping keeps each day neatly separated. Each section of the app — Timeline, Favorites, albums, locations, and cloud archive — can use its own grouping, so you can keep the Timeline by month while browsing Favorites by year.
Open Settings > Timeline & albums to choose the grouping method and toggle date separators independently for each screen. You can also turn off date headers entirely for a clean, continuous grid.
Pick a group method for each screen
Choose none, daily, monthly, or yearly
Toggle date separators on or off
Export the embedded clip from a motion photo as a standalone video

Motion photos store a short video alongside the still image. When you open one, Gallery shows a \'Save as video\' action that exports the embedded clip as a regular MP4 in your Movies folder — perfect for sharing the moment as a video. This works on local, non-encrypted motion photos.
Find the new clip in your Movies folder
Posterize, Edges, Borders, Hue, and black & white

The editor now has an Effects tab with creative tools: Posterize reduces the photo to flat bands of colour, Edges traces outlines for a sketch-like look, and Borders adds a frame around the image. Each effect renders a live preview so you can dial it in before saving.

In the Colour tab you can shift the overall Hue or convert to black & white with GPU-accelerated previews. The Filters grid also includes a one-tap Negative preset that inverts colours instantly. Combine effects, colour tools, filters, and adjustments for a fully custom look.
Open a photo and tap edit
Open the Effects or Colour tab
Adjust the effect and preview live
Group albums into collapsible categories like Common, Apps, and Other

When enabled, Gallery automatically sorts your albums into collapsible sections — Common (Camera, Pictures, Downloads), Apps (media from other apps), and Other — based on where they\'re stored. Collapse the sections you don\'t need to keep the Albums screen tidy. This is off by default and can be enabled in album settings.
Move an album to a section
Sections are auto-detected, but you can override them. Long press an album and choose \'Move to section\' to place it exactly where you want. Your manual choices are remembered.
Pick the section for that album
Export and import settings, favorites, cloud configs, and vaults

The backup & restore wizard lets you export your settings, favorites, cloud server configs, and vaults into a single archive you can move to a new device or keep safe. You can optionally protect the whole archive with a password. Vault backup is off by default and requires your vault credential before any vault is included.
Choose exactly what to include. If you enable vaults, you select which ones, and each is authenticated with its own credential at export time so nothing leaves your device unlocked by accident.
Choose Export and pick what to include
Save the backup file to a safe place
Importing lets you pick which parts of the archive to restore. Select the backup file, enter the password if one was set, then choose the data and vaults to bring back.
Choose Import and select your backup file
Enter the password if the archive is encrypted
Pick the data and vaults to restore
Automatically hardens Gallery when device-wide Advanced Protection is on

On Android 16 and later, if you turn on Advanced Protection in your device security settings, Gallery automatically raises its own defenses — forcing sandboxed image decoding and stronger metadata isolation. This protects against malicious images and files without any extra setup.

While Advanced Protection is active, the related security toggles are locked on and shown as enforced. Your previous choices are remembered and restored automatically if you ever turn Advanced Protection off.
Sign in to your Immich server to bring remote photos into Gallery

Gallery can connect to your self-hosted Immich server so your remote library lives right alongside your device photos. Sign in with your email and password or an API key — credentials are stored encrypted on your device. You can connect more than one server and switch between them.
You\'ll need your server URL and either your account password or an API key. Once connected, Gallery validates the session and keeps it signed in, reconnecting automatically when needed.
Open Cloud accounts from settings or Library
Add a server and enter its URL
Sign in with password or API key
Your remote photos appear in the timeline
Remote and local media merge into one unified timeline

Once a server is connected, your cloud assets are merged into the same timeline as your device photos, sorted by date. Photos that exist both locally and on the server are intelligently grouped so you don\'t see duplicates. A small icon on each thumbnail shows its sync status.

Open any cloud photo or video to view it full quality, streamed from your server. You can see its full EXIF metadata — camera, lens, GPS, exposure — and favorite or unfavorite it just like local media.
Upload selected albums with Wi-Fi and cellular controls

Choose which local albums to back up to your server and let Gallery upload them in the background. A checksum-based deduplication check avoids re-uploading photos that already exist on the server, saving time and bandwidth.
Set separate Wi-Fi and cellular rules for photos and videos so backups only run when you want. Background uploads are scheduled periodically, and you can watch progress on the backup details screen.
Pick the albums to back up
Browse cloud people, map, smart search, and shared links

A connected server unlocks more ways to browse: see recognized people and open all their photos, explore geo-tagged media on a map, and view albums shared with you. You can even edit a person\'s name and birthday right from Gallery.

Use CLIP-based smart search to find photos by describing them in natural language. You can also create shareable links for assets and albums, then list, edit, or delete them — all from within Gallery.
Connect your self-hosted Immich server and browse remote photos in a unified timeline with people, map, and smart search
Back up selected albums to your server with deduplication and Wi-Fi/cellular controls
Export and import settings, favorites, cloud configs, and vaults, with optional password encryption
Posterize, Edges, Borders, Hue, black & white, and a one-tap Negative preset
Group media by day, month, or year — configurable per screen
Organize albums into collapsible Common, Apps, and Other sections
Export the embedded clip from a motion photo as a standalone MP4
Automatically hardens decoding and metadata isolation when Android Advanced Protection is on
Improved animated APNG playback and JPEG XL image support
Faster grid rendering, image loading paused while fast-scrolling, prefetching for flicker-free transitions, and a new Sketch-based viewer pipeline
Tap a photo\'s location to see a map preview with quick actions to open it in app or externally
Fixed panorama mirroring, background audio on focus loss, picker album thumbnails, file descriptor leaks, vault UX issues, duplicate-key crashes, and SD card copy/move destinations
